description: S98 REAL-UI input upgrade of the auto-download size-limit field (NOVEL, UNVALIDATED, nonBlocking): enter_text into the real Settings size-limit TextField (UiKeys.settingsDownloadLimitField = 'settings_download_limit_field' @ lib/ui/settings/global_settings_section.dart:611) then tap the Save button (UiKeys.settingsDownloadLimitSaveButton = 'settings_download_limit_save_button' @ :640), and prove autoDownloadSizeLimit flips in l3_dump_state (30->13), then re-enter 30 + Save to restore. THREE BLOCKERS make this nonBlocking and OWE live-validation + codex-review: (1) NO existing scenario uses the tap/enter_text actions — this is a novel, never-run pattern; (2) the marionette ext (ext.flutter.marionette.tap / .enterText) vs working binding is unresolved (MCP_BINDING=skill lacks the marionette ext; MCP_BINDING=marionette can hang startup); (3) the runner has no scroll action and BOTH the field and Save button sit well below the settings-page fold (Auto Download Size Limit card), so the input/tap may fail to hit a laid-out widget. The PROVEN data-half is l3_download_limit_toggle.json (S87) — that hard gate drives l3_set_setting autoDownloadSizeLimit (int MB) and is what actually passes today. The proper executable form may be a flutter test WidgetTester gate (REAL_UI_GATES recipe), not this JSON. The save handler validates 1..10000 MB (lib/ui/settings/global_settings_section.dart:167); 13 and 30 are both in range. Soft precondition (starts 30, the macOS seeded default) catches a dirty fixture; ends restored to 30 so the fixture self-cleans ON SUCCESSFUL COMPLETION only (no `teardown` phase — a mid-run failure/interruption can leave the setting flipped; re-run or restore manually).
